DSDS 0.1
Overview Root Schema
accessibility artifact-reference example extensions guideline link metadata rich-text status usecase
anatomy api component design-specifications state variant
token-api token-value token
style
pattern

DSDS Example Definitions

A unified example model used across DSDS. Each example requires a presentation — a visual or interactive demonstration. Optional title, description, and value properties provide context. Presentations are independent typed objects for images, videos, code snippets, and Storybook stories.

Source: common/example.schema.json

5 definitions in this file:

  • presentationImage
  • presentationVideo
  • presentationCode
  • presentationUrl
  • example

presentationImage

An image presentation — a screenshot, diagram, annotated mockup, or exported design frame.

object

PropertyTypeRequiredDescription
type"image"requiredIdentifies this presentation as an image.
urlstring (uri)requiredURL or relative path to the image file.
altstringrequiredAlternative text describing the content of the image. MUST be provided for accessibility.

Example

{
  "type": "image",
  "url": "https://design.acme.com/assets/button-primary-default.png",
  "alt": "A primary button in its default state, with a solid blue background and white label text reading Save."
}

presentationVideo

A video presentation — a screen recording, animated transition, or interaction walkthrough.

object

PropertyTypeRequiredDescription
type"video"requiredIdentifies this presentation as a video.
urlstring (uri)requiredURL or relative path to the video file.
altstringrequiredAlternative text describing the content of the video. MUST be provided for accessibility.

Example

{
  "type": "video",
  "url": "https://design.acme.com/assets/button-loading-interaction.mp4",
  "alt": "A user clicks a primary button labeled Save. The label is replaced by a spinner animation while the button maintains its width. After two seconds, the spinner is replaced by a checkmark and the label changes to Saved."
}

presentationCode

A code presentation — a source code snippet showing how to use the artifact.

object

PropertyTypeRequiredDescription
type"code"requiredIdentifies this presentation as a code snippet.
codestringrequiredThe source code of the example.
languagestringrequiredThe programming language or syntax (e.g., 'jsx', 'tsx', 'html', 'css', 'vue', 'svelte', 'swift', 'kotlin').

Example

{
  "type": "code",
  "code": "<ButtonGroup>\n  <Button variant=\"secondary\">Cancel</Button>\n  <Button variant=\"primary\">Save</Button>\n</ButtonGroup>",
  "language": "jsx"
}

presentationUrl

A URL presentation — a link to any web resource such as a live demo, interactive example, documentation page, or hosted component story. Use for Storybook stories, CodeSandbox embeds, StackBlitz projects, or any other addressable web content.

object

PropertyTypeRequiredDescription
type"url"requiredIdentifies this presentation as a URL reference to a web resource.
urlstring (uri)requiredURL to the web resource (e.g., 'https://storybook.acme.com/?path=/story/components-button--primary', 'https://codesandbox.io/s/button-example').

Example

{
  "type": "url",
  "url": "https://storybook.acme.com/?path=/story/components-button--primary"
}

example

A single example. Every example requires a presentation — a visual or interactive demonstration of the concept. The optional title and description provide context. The optional value property carries a literal value for API property examples.

object

PropertyTypeRequiredDescription
presentationpresentationImage | presentationVideo | presentationCode | presentationUrlrequiredThe visual or interactive presentation for the example.
titlestringoptionalA human-readable caption or title for the example. This is a display label (e.g., 'Primary button in default state'). For API property examples, use `value` for the literal content and `title` for the human-readable label.
descriptionstringoptionalAn explanation of the example — why it is encouraged, discouraged, or notable.
valueanyoptionalA literal value carried by the example. Used for API property examples where the content is a concrete value rather than a visual demonstration. Accepts any JSON type: string (e.g., 'primary'), number (e.g., 44), boolean (e.g., true), object, array, or null. Do not use `value` for display text — use `title` for captions and `description` for explanations.

References: presentationImage, presentationVideo, presentationCode, presentationUrl

Example

[
  {
    "title": "Primary button — default state",
    "description": "The high-emphasis button style used for the most important action on a surface.",
    "presentation": {
      "type": "image",
      "url": "https://design.acme.com/assets/button-primary-default.png",
      "alt": "A primary button with a solid blue background and white label text reading Save."
    }
  },
  {
    "title": "JSX — button group with primary and secondary",
    "presentation": {
      "type": "code",
      "code": "<ButtonGroup>\n  <Button variant=\"secondary\">Cancel</Button>\n  <Button variant=\"primary\">Save</Button>\n</ButtonGroup>",
      "language": "jsx"
    }
  },
  {
    "title": "Loading state transition",
    "description": "Shows the full loading lifecycle from click through completion.",
    "presentation": {
      "type": "video",
      "url": "https://design.acme.com/assets/button-loading-interaction.mp4",
      "alt": "A user clicks Save. The label is replaced by a spinner. After two seconds, the spinner becomes a checkmark and the label changes to Saved."
    }
  },
  {
    "title": "Interactive primary button story",
    "presentation": {
      "type": "url",
      "url": "https://storybook.acme.com/?path=/story/components-button--primary"
    }
  },
  {
    "title": "variant property — primary",
    "value": "primary",
    "presentation": {
      "type": "code",
      "code": "<Button variant=\"primary\">Save</Button>",
      "language": "jsx"
    }
  }
]
↑ Back to top
